commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This needs maintainer product/security review before any automated repair lane should take over the branch.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a new network listener and shared HMAC secret path that need hardening before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Bind the Teams media socket to an explicit safe host вЂ” extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.ts:149 - [P1] Move Teams media startup and shutdown into.... (sha=450015659240e6db6bf70cc22fa93f55103db6e5)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Microsoft Teams voice-call provider with an HMAC-authenticated WebSocket bridge, PCM STT/TTS wiring, config/schema entries, and provider/transport tests.

PR surface: Source +1117, Tests +674. Total +1791 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection of the PR head shows the omitted listen host, unawaited media server startup, missing runtime stop hook, and missing msteams validation/SecretRef contract. Original bug reproduction is not applicable because this PR adds a new provider capability.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs maintainer product/security review before any automated repair lane should take over the branch.

target.title: feat(voice-call): add Microsoft Teams voice provider (OpenClawTeamsBridge)
target.body:
п»ї## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
- OpenClaw's `voice-call` extension supports Twilio, Telnyx, Plivo, and a mock provider, but has no way to handle **Microsoft Teams** calls. Teams audio cannot reach the existing webhook/media-stream plane because Teams calls are delivered through Graph Calling signalling + the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket, which only run on .NET/Windows.
- This adds a 5th voice-call provider, **`msteams`**, that bridges Teams call audio into the existing voice-call machinery (CallManager, realtime transcription, response generation, telephony TTS).

**Why does this matter now?**
- It unlocks real-time voice agents on Microsoft Teams using the same OpenClaw agent/STT/TTS stack already used for PSTN providers, with no changes to those providers.

**What is the intended outcome?**
- An operator can set `provider: "msteams"` and OpenClaw will accept per-call WebSocket connections from an external Windows worker ("OpenClawTeamsBridge" / AzureBot), authenticate them, and run the full inboundГўвЂ вЂ™STTГўвЂ вЂ™agentГўвЂ вЂ™TTSГўвЂ вЂ™outbound loop with barge-in Гўв‚¬вЂќ mirroring the streaming Twilio provider but speaking **PCM 16 kHz** instead of mu-law 8 kHz.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
- The Windows-side worker (Graph Calling notification endpoint + `Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket) is a **separate component, not included in this repo**. This PR only adds the OpenClaw-side provider + WebSocket transport it connects to.
- Outbound dialing (initiating Teams calls) Гўв‚¬вЂќ the provider is inbound-only and throws on `initiateCall`.

**What does success look like?**
- `provider: "msteams"` binds an HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; a live Teams call routed through the external worker produces a transcript, an agent reply, and synthesized speech back to the caller, with barge-in.

**What should reviewers focus on?**
- The **WebSocket auth/transport** in `msteams-media-stream.ts` (HMAC verification, replay window, connection bounds).
- The two **config validation behavior changes** for `msteams` in `config.ts` (see Risk).
- That the new provider reuses existing exported helpers (`generateVoiceResponse`, `CallManager.speak`, telephony TTS) rather than duplicating webhook logic.

---

## Linked context

**Which issue does this close?**

Closes # <!-- fill in if a tracking issue exists; otherwise remove -->

**Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?**

Related # <!-- e.g. prior Telnyx/Twilio realtime streaming PRs this mirrors -->

**Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?**

<!-- Note any maintainer/owner request here, or state "Community contribution." -->

---

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `voice-call` had no Microsoft Teams provider Гўв‚¬вЂќ `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as unsupported. This PR adds the provider, the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket transport, and the inboundГўвЂ вЂ™STTГўвЂ вЂ™agentГўвЂ вЂ™TTSГўвЂ вЂ™outbound pipeline.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 local gateway on Windows Server (Azure VM); `voice-call` provider `msteams`; agent `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`; ElevenLabs realtime STT (`scribe_v2_realtime`, `pcm_16000`) + ElevenLabs TTS; an external Windows worker bridged a real inbound Microsoft Teams call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build`
  2. `node openclaw.mjs config validate`  ГўвЂ вЂ™ prints `Config valid`
  3. `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --force`
  4. Placed a real Microsoft Teams call to the bot, spoke several turns out loud, heard the bot's spoken replies, then hung up.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  (a) Gateway lifecycle log during the call (private identifiers redacted):
  ```
  MsteamsMediaStream listening port=8443 path=/voice/msteams/stream
  [voice-call] msteams realtime transcription provider: elevenlabs
  [voice-call] msteams TTS provider configured
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection open <callId>
  MsteamsProvider: session.start callId=<callId> threadId=<threadId> caller.aadId=<aadId>
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed <callId>
  ```
  (b) Agent session trajectory for the same call (`sessionKey: voice:<redacted>`, model `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`) Гўв‚¬вЂќ the inbound caller audio was transcribed by ElevenLabs STT and answered by the agent; each `{"spoken":...}` reply is what was synthesized back over the bridge as TTS the caller heard:
  ```
  user  (STT): "How are you, man?"
  asst (spoken): "IГўв‚¬в„ўm doing well, man Гўв‚¬вЂќ thanks. What can I help you with?"
  user  (STT): "Tell me more about you."
  asst (spoken): "IГўв‚¬в„ўm your phone assistant in OpenClaw Гўв‚¬вЂќ calm, practical, and here to help with
                  questions, quick research, reminders, messages, and getting things doneГўв‚¬В¦"
  user  (STT): "Thank you. And, uh, what is your-"
  asst (spoken): "IГўв‚¬в„ўm an AI phone assistant running in OpenClaw. I donГўв‚¬в„ўt have a body or a personal
                  life, but I can talk with you, look things up, help you planГўв‚¬В¦"
  user  (STT): "Mm-hmm"
  asst (spoken): "Yeah Гўв‚¬вЂќ kind of like a calm digital helper you can just talk toГўв‚¬В¦"
  ```
- Observed result after fix: full bidirectional audio round-trip on a live Teams call Гўв‚¬вЂќ caller speech ГўвЂ вЂ™ ElevenLabs STT transcript ГўвЂ вЂ™ `gpt-5.4` agent reply ГўвЂ вЂ™ ElevenLabs TTS played back to the caller, across multiple turns over ~70 s. The speech disfluencies ("uh", "Mm-hmm") and natural turn-taking confirm these are live ASR transcripts (not typed input); the `{"spoken":...}` payloads are exactly what the provider sends to TTS. Session lifecycle (`connection open` / `session.start` / `connection closed`) is verified in the gateway log.
- What was not tested: outbound dialing (the provider is inbound-only and out of scope); sustained barge-in and high-concurrency load have not yet been captured under real calls.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: transcript/reply text is taken from the agent session trajectory because the gateway file log does not record transcript text at info level. Private identifiers (callId, threadId, caller aadId, sessionKey) are redacted.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, `provider: "msteams"` produced `Unsupported voice-call provider: msteams`.

---

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts \
  extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts
node openclaw.mjs config validate
pnpm build      # clean compile of the changed extension into dist
```
Result:
```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```
`node openclaw.mjs config validate` ГўвЂ вЂ™ `Config valid`.

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
- `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts` Гўв‚¬вЂќ provider lifecycle: session.start ГўвЂ вЂ™ CallManager registration, transcript ГўвЂ вЂ™ response, barge-in (`assistant.cancel`), playTts framing/pacing, hangup/teardown.
- `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts` Гўв‚¬вЂќ WebSocket server: HMAC upgrade verification (accept/reject), replay-window rejection, `session.start`/`session.end`/`audio.frame`/`ping` parsing, outbound `send`/`close`, and **inbound payload-cap rejection** (oversized frame ГўвЂ вЂ™ close `1009`, added for GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5).

**What failed before this fix, if known?**
- `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as an unsupported voice-call provider; there was no transport for Teams audio.

**If no test was added, why not?** N/A Гўв‚¬вЂќ unit tests added for both the provider and the transport.

---

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes (additive, opt-in)` Гўв‚¬вЂќ adds a new `msteams` provider option; existing Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo/mock behavior is unchanged. For msteams specifically, `inboundPolicy` now **defaults to `"open"`** (Teams is inbound-first), so msteams calls are accepted unless the operator sets an explicit policy.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `Yes` Гўв‚¬вЂќ adds `voice-call` config under `provider: "msteams"` (`msteams.{port,path,sharedSecret}`, plus `streaming` and `tts` blocks). Two `msteams`-scoped validation changes in `config.ts`:
1. `msteams` is exempt from the `fromNumber` requirement (it never places outbound PSTN calls).
2. `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"` for `msteams` when unset.
No data migration.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `Yes` Гўв‚¬вЂќ introduces a **WebSocket server** (operator-configured port) that accepts connections from the external worker.

**What is the highest-risk area?** The WebSocket transport (`msteams-media-stream.ts`) Гўв‚¬вЂќ it listens on a network port and accepts external connections carrying call audio.

**How is that risk mitigated?**
- Every HTTP upgrade is authenticated with **HMAC-SHA256** over `timestamp + callId` using a shared secret, compared in constant time (`safeEqualSecret`).
- **Replay protection:** upgrades whose timestamp drifts beyond a configurable window (default 60 s) are rejected.
- **DoS bounds:** pre-start timeout drops unauthenticated idle sockets; caps on total / pending / per-IP connections.
- Inbound message bodies are validated with a strict `zod` discriminated union.
- **Operator guidance:** bind the WS to a trusted network segment (e.g., Tailscale / private interface), not a public `0.0.0.0` surface. The shared secret must match the worker's and never be committed.

---

## Current review state

**What is the next action?**
- Maintainer review of the WebSocket auth/transport and the two msteams config-validation changes.

**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?**
- **External proof:** end-to-end live-audio capture, pending the external Windows worker's `audio.frame` streaming.

**Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?**
- **GitHub Advanced Security (GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5):** the msteams WebSocket server now sets a 64 KB `maxPayload`, rejecting oversized inbound frames before parsing; added a regression test (oversized frame ГўвЂ вЂ™ close `1009`).
- Internal worker/product names and phased-task references were removed from comments for upstream.

**Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?** None yet Гўв‚¬вЂќ initial submission.

---

## Files changed (feature scope)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.ts` | New `msteams` provider: WS lifecycle wiring, inbound audio ГўвЂ вЂ™ STT, response generation, TTS playback with paced PCM framing, barge-in, CallManager integration. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts` | Provider unit tests. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.ts` | HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; session.start/end/audio.frame/ping protocol; outbound send/close. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts` | Transport unit tests. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-tts.ts` | Teams PCM TTS helper. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams.runtime.ts` | Resolves the realtime-transcription provider + telephony TTS and injects CallManager/response runtime for msteams. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/telephony-tts.ts` | Adds `synthesizePcmForTelephony` (raw PCM, for the 16 kHz Teams bridge), refactored out of the shared mu-law path. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.ts` | Hooks msteams streaming wiring into `createVoiceCallRuntime`. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts` | `MsteamsConfigSchema` (`port`/`path`/`sharedSecret`); msteams exempt from `fromNumber`; `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"` for msteams. |
target.author: ahenawy
